title: Deus Ex: Game of the Year Edition

notes: Game worked perfectly out of the box. Tested both DirectX and OpenGL renderers.
title: Deus Ex: Game of the Year Edition

notes:
The gamma slider in game does not function (and the game is very VERY dark).  Due to this, there are many reports of the game being too dark to play.

As a workaround, I highly recommend manually adjusting gamma before starting the game by using xgamma in the terminal (e.g. $ xgamma -gamma 1.8).  After you are finished playing, you can reset gamma to "normal" via "$ xgamma -gamma 1.0".

Occasionally, starting in full screen will fail to fill the entire screen -- showing a 1px wide view of the desktop along the bottom and right edges.  This can be fixed be entering the in-game settings, disabling Full Screen, and then re-enabling Full Screen.

Otherwise, only the occasional stuck sound (infinite 1 second loop), which requires a game restart.  Overall, runs great aside from the primary gamma issue and the other minor annoyances mentioned.
title: Deus Ex: Game of the Year Edition

notes: Runs perfectly. Highly recommended to use the updated OpenGL renderer found here https://www.cwdohnal.com/utglr/ Just drop the .dll file into the game's system folder and overwrite the existing file.
title: Deus Ex: Game of the Year Edition

notes: Runs mostly fine except for the darkness problem. I tested all of the renderers that were shown in  Kenties launcher and the only renderer that didn't have this problem is the Glide renderer, but that looked bad and had other issues. You'll have to turn up the gamma in X in order to see anything.

notes: Like others have said below, everything works except for the brightness slider, making the game way too dark to be playable. Use the xgamma command to work around this issue. The sweet spot for me is 1.4.
